page: Amazon Web Services r8gn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated physical cores powered by the 64-bit ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. The CPU architecture allocates one thread per core, providing 96 vCPUs with 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, but features a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the r8gn.24xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core capabilities. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression both rank in the top 10% tier. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, with read-only small block, read-only large block, and write large block tests all scoring in the top 10%. Database operations, including Redis SET operations and PassMark database tests, also achieve top-tier placement. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core results and Geekbench text processing falling into the middle 50% average.

Large language model inference speeds show distinct tradeoffs. While text generation for medium (7B) and large (70B) models ranks in the top 10% tier, text generation for small (135M) models is relatively weak, falling into the bottom 25%. This instance is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, multi-threaded data processing, and static web serving, where its 300 Gbps network bandwidth and high memory density provide significant resource efficiency.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.24xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance powered by 96 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating at 2.8 GHz, it features 768.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB per core, alongside a 300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core execution speeds remain in the average tier. Tradeoffs are present in LLM inference, where text generation for medium and large models ranks in the top 10%, while small model text generation falls into the bottom 25%. This instance is optimized for memory-intensive applications, database deployments, and high-throughput network workloads.

768.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
